articles.title: CONSIDERING STARTING A COMPANY OR JOINING YOUR NEXT THING?
articles.summary: Apply for Next Play [5] (totally free!)—we pair good people who are thinking about what comes next, anywhere in the world, as a way to spark ideas, build momentum, and chat about what may come next.

articles.title: REDDIT FILES FOR IPO AND INVITES POWER USERS TO INVEST
articles.summary: Reddit has filed for an initial public offering (IPO) with the SEC. The filing revealed that it made $804 million in revenue last year, mostly from advertising, while incurring a net loss of $90.8 million. In an unconventional move, Reddit is offering a portion of its shares to some of its most dedicated users, including moderators and those with high karma scores.

articles.title: BENCHMARKS FOR FOUNDER SALARY AND BURN RATE BY STAGE
articles.summary: Comprehensive insights into founder compensation, employee stock option pools (ESOP), burn rates, and team size by stage. Recent data shows early-stage companies are burning way less cash monthly than during 2021's ZIRP era. While $20M+ high-growth ventures might be burning $1M+ per month, more modest sub-$5M startups now operate on $50K or less.

articles.title: THE STATE OF SAAS PRICING
articles.summary: Is usage-based pricing still the future of SaaS pricing or does it make companies too susceptible to market uncertainties and changes in customer behavior? This article aims to examine the various pricing models adopted by startups and evaluates their effectiveness.

articles.title: BE MORE ASSERTIVE WITH YOUR CUSTOMERS
articles.summary: It’s easy to feel you're pestering customers, but being afraid to speak up can lead to lost opportunities and revenue. As founders, we’re serving customers with broader industry insights that they lack. It’s okay to be assertive, push back, and say no to certain requests, as long as you’re not aggressive.

articles.title: WINNING STRATEGIES FOR APPLIED AI COMPANIES
articles.summary: We are currently experiencing the third wave of AI. The first wave was research-driven AI. This was followed by the development of ML infrastructure, and now we are in the era of applied AI. Applied AI refers to SaaS companies that are utilizing AI to significantly enhance their products. This article delves into more than 70 applied AI companies that have raised over $7 million in VC funding, highlighting how they are growing and why.

articles.title: THE BEST WAY TO DRIVE DEMAND IN MARKETPLACES
articles.summary: In marketplace businesses, the network is the product, and growing both supply and demand is essential for the product to improve over time. Driving between 10% and 40% of demand from supply is the "Goldilocks zone" for maximizing the value of a marketplace's supply to grow demand.

articles.title: MINDY
articles.summary: Email-based Chief of Staff powered by AI.

articles.title: EMBED (ACCELERATOR)
articles.summary: An Accelerator for AI-Native Startups. Deadline is March 1st.

articles.title: HF0 (ACCELERATOR)
articles.summary: This is a program for repeat founders. Deadline is February 25th.

articles.title: PERFECTING THE PITCH
articles.summary: This article provides a pitch framework composed of five elements: Timing, Pitch, Emotion, Signals, and Assessment. The framework emphasizes the importance of timing in making a good first impression and building credibility. It highlights the need for a concise and engaging pitch that effectively communicates the market, opportunity, product, team, and milestones.

articles.title: TURNS OUT, GOOGLE IS ALL ABOUT LINKS AND CLICKS
articles.summary: Google is showing more and more bad pages in its search results. Its search engine is not able to easily see this content as spam like a human - it still struggles to evaluate quality content. Google continues to rely on clicks and links to tune rankings, possibly now more than ever.

articles.title: HOW NETFLIX BUILDS A CULTURE OF EXCELLENCE (73 MINUTE AUDIO)
articles.summary: Elizabeth Stone, the CTO of Netflix, talks through how and why Netflix maintains a high bar for excellence and specifically how it has fostered that internally through its culture with clear communication.

articles.title: HOW DO INVESTORS FEEL ABOUT ACQUIHIRES?
articles.summary: Investors' sentiment about "acquihires" has evolved over the years. VCs initially worked with acquihires to provide a "soft landing" for struggling startups, even if the returns were minimal. However, VCs today generally don't prioritize acquihires as they don't want to invest energy in getting minimal returns.

articles.title: BREAKING THE J-CURVE
articles.summary: Venture capital math is smart in theory, but stupid in practice. It’s easy to get caught in metrics like the ideal LTV/CAC ratio. However, be skeptical of rigid J-curve formulas when trying to build a sustainable, growing business long-term. Find an unfair, low-cost source of pipeline that gets you out of the J-curve game.

articles.title: YOU WANT TO HEAR ADVICE THAT STINGS
articles.summary: Startups can be very kumbaya at times - people don’t want to speak hard truths. However, the truth is exactly what you need to hear. Find people who give it to you straight and cherish them.
